assumptions: Assume the declared intragroup dividend gives rise to a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup receivable/payable.
Assume the question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability and also addresses qualifying intragroup foreign-currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a separate hedge accounting model for foreign-currency risk relating to a net investment in a foreign operation in consolidated financial statements.
